Document:
[color=#897236][mono]──── [color=#FFA500][bold]II. Smuggling[/bold][/color] ───────────────────────────────[/mono][/color]
The Triad Sector uses an advanced Contraband Detection System integrated into mass scanners for easy detection of smugglers in the sector. Security personnel are required to follow the procedure dictated below for lawful interdiction and confiscation of contraband:
[head=3][color=#FFA500]1. Detection[/color][/head] - [bold]Identifying:[/bold] Vessels carrying illegal contraband will show a Red Blip Signature inside their ship. - [bold]Prioritising:[/bold] TDF patrols should prioritize ships heading toward trade hubs (Trade Mall/Cargo Depots) or ships already docked there. - [bold]Probable Cause:[/bold] A red, squared-shaped blip can only mean illegal cargo is inside the vessel and is sufficient to interdict and search a ship with no further paperwork.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]2. Interception Protocol[/color][/head] - [bold]Hailing:[/bold] Hail the vessel, reach out to them via Shortband, then contact via holopad if they do not respond to Shortband. State requirement for the pilot to power down engines and prepare the ship for inspection. - [bold]Position:[/bold] Position TDF vessels to block the escape vector of the ship or stay within FTL lock range of the ship.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]3. Deterrence & Seizure[/color][/head] - [bold]Locating:[/bold] Hail the vessel, reach out to them via Shortband, then contact via holopad if they do not respond to Shortband. State requirement for the pilot to power down engines and prepare the ship for inspection. - [bold]First Offences:[/bold] Smugglers are to be let go if they are first time offenders, with all contraband seized and properly secured. - [bold]Reoffenders:[/bold] For first recidivism incidents, they are to be fined 50,000 credits for each piece of contraband seized. Further incidents are subject to detainment and withheld with allotted time based on applicable laws of contraband smuggling.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]4. Handling Contraband[/color][/head] - Contraband that is seized by security or law enforcement is to be sent to the Auric Outpost and exchanged for TCC on their sell pallet. Money obtained from the selling and seizing of the contraband are to be split 50/50, half to the outpost's bank and the other half to the Enforcers which participated in the confiscation. - [bold][color=red]Any Enforcer who pockets more than the 50% allotted reward, will be brought to questioning for disciplinary actions by Triad Defense Force Command. Repeated action of this behaviour will result in the permanent discharge of said Enforcer.[/color][/bold]
NEXT PAGE --> [textlink="III. Belligerent Vessel's Aggression & Naval Engagements" link="SOPNavalEngagements"]
